# services.zapret

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.zapret`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.zapret.blacklist` | `list of string` | Specify a list of domains NOT to bypass. All other domains will be bypassed. You can specify either whitelist or blacklist, but not both. If neither are specified, then bypass all domains. |
| `services.zapret.configureFirewall` | `boolean` | Whether to setup firewall routing so that system http(s) traffic is forwarded via this service. Disable if you want to set it up manually. |
| `services.zapret.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the Zapret DPI bypass service.. |
| `services.zapret.httpMode` | `one of "first", "full"` | By default this service only changes the first packet sent, which is enough in most cases. But there are DPIs that monitor the whole traffic within a session. That requires full processing of every packet, which increases the CPU usage. Set the mode to `full` if http doesn't work. |
| `services.zapret.httpSupport` | `boolean` | Whether to route http traffic on port 80. Http bypass rarely works and you might want to disable it if you don't utilise http connections. |
| `services.zapret.package` | `package` | The zapret package to use. |
| `services.zapret.params` | `list of string` | Specify the bypass parameters for Zapret binary. There are no universal parameters as they vary between different networks, so you'll have to find them yourself. This can be done by running the `blockcheck` binary from zapret package, i.e. `nix-shell -p nftables zapret --command blockcheck` (or `iptables` instead of `nftables` if that is what your firewall is using). It'll try different params and then tell you which params are working for your network. |
| `services.zapret.qnum` | `signed integer` | Routing queue number. Only change this if you already use the default queue number somewhere else. |
| `services.zapret.udpPorts` | `list of string` | List of UDP ports to route. Port ranges are delimited with a colon like this "50000:50099". |
| `services.zapret.udpSupport` | `boolean` | Enable UDP routing. This requires you to specify `udpPorts` and `--dpi-desync-any-protocol` parameter. |
| `services.zapret.whitelist` | `list of string` | Specify a list of domains to bypass. All other domains will be ignored. You can specify either whitelist or blacklist, but not both. If neither are specified, then bypass all domains. It is recommended to specify the whitelist. This will make sure that other resources won't be affected by this service. |
